CO:boulder.council_note: All seats nonpartisan. Next election: Nov 2027.

FL:miami.council_note: Strong mayor is chief executive. Nov 2025 general election held.

FL:tampa.council_note: Mayor Jane Castor serves as chief executive.

FL:orlando.council_note: Mayor Buddy Dyer serves as chief executive.

FL:tallahassee.council_structure: 5 commissioners (all elected citywide); Mayor chosen from among commissioners
FL:tallahassee.council_note: City Manager serves as chief administrative officer.

FL:st-petersburg.council_note: Mayor Kenneth T. Welch (sworn in Jan 2022).

IL:chicago.council_note: Nonpartisan; all 50 seats up every 4 years. Next: 2027.

MN:st-paul.council_note: All 7 councilmembers are women. Next election: Nov 2028.
MN:st-paul.city_hall_address: 15 W. Kellogg Blvd., Saint Paul, MN 55102

NY:new-york-city.council_structure: 51 members, one per district across 5 boroughs
NY:new-york-city.city_hall_address: City Hall Park, New York, NY 10007

TN:nashville.council_structure: 35 district members + 5 at-large; Vice Mayor presides

TN:memphis.council_note: Next election: Oct 2027. 4-year terms with 2-term limit.

TX:san-antonio.city_hall_address: 114 W. Commerce St., San Antonio, TX 78205

ID:boise.council_structure: 6 members elected from 6 districts; mayor elected citywide

UT:salt-lake-city.council_structure: 7 members elected from 7 districts; mayor elected separately
UT:salt-lake-city.council_note: Nonpartisan; Ale Puy elected Council Chair for 2026.

MT:missoula.council_structure: 12 members, 2 per ward from 6 wards; mayor elected citywide
MT:missoula.council_note: Nonpartisan; Mayor Andrea Davis won full term Nov 2025.

MT:bozeman.council_note: Nonpartisan; government study commission elected Nov 2024 to review charter.
MT:bozeman.meeting_frequency: Every Tue (except after holidays and 5th Tue)

AZ:tucson.council_structure: Mayor + 6 council members from 6 wards; mayor elected at-large; only AZ city with partisan elections
AZ:tucson.council_note: Democratic mayor Regina Romero; partisan elections.

AZ:phoenix.council_structure: Mayor + 8 district council members; mayor elected at-large, members by district
AZ:phoenix.council_note: Nonpartisan; Mayor Kate Gallego; largest Council-Manager city in US.

AZ:flagstaff.council_structure: Mayor + 6 council members, all elected at-large

IA:des-moines.charter_status: Home Rule (council-manager-ward form under Iowa Code)
IA:des-moines.council_structure: Mayor + 2 at-large + 4 ward representatives; city manager appointed by council
IA:des-moines.city_hall_name: T.M. Franklin Cownie City Administration Building

IA:iowa-city.council_structure: 7 members: 4 at-large + 3 district; mayor chosen by council from its members
IA:iowa-city.council_note: Nonpartisan; next election Nov 2025 (1 district + 2 at-large seats).
IA:iowa-city.city_hall_address: 410 E Washington St, Iowa City, IA 52240

MI:ann-arbor.council_structure: Mayor + 10 members (2 per ward, 5 wards); partisan elections; staggered 4-year terms
MI:ann-arbor.city_hall_name: Guy C. Larcom Jr. Municipal Building
MI:ann-arbor.city_hall_address: 301 E Huron St, Ann Arbor, MI 48104

MN:duluth.council_structure: 5 district + 4 at-large councilors; mayor elected separately
MN:duluth.council_note: Nonpartisan; Mayor Roger Reinert elected Nov 2023.

ND:fargo.council_structure: Mayor + 4 commissioners, all elected at-large; city administrator appointed
ND:fargo.council_note: Nonpartisan; Mayor Mahoney; considering expansion to 7 members (2026 ballot).

MA:boston.council_note: Nonpartisan elections. Council president serves as acting mayor when mayor is absent.
MA:boston.city_hall_address: 1 City Hall Square, 5th Floor, Boston, MA 02201

MA:cambridge.council_note: Mayor and Vice Mayor elected by council from its members. City Manager is chief executive.

MA:northampton.charter_status: Home Rule Charter (adopted 2012, Acts of 2012 Ch. 277)
MA:northampton.council_note: Council president elected by council. Mayor serves 4-year term as chief executive.

MA:somerville.council_note: Formerly Board of Aldermen. Most densely populated city in New England.

CT:new-haven.council_note: Legislative body called Board of Alders. President elected from among members.

CT:hamden.charter_status: Town Charter (Annotated Charter of the Town of Hamden)
CT:hamden.council_note: Town government. First 4-year mayoral term began 2025. At-large voters choose up to 4 of 6 seats.

ME:portland.council_structure: Mayor (at-large, 4-yr term) + 5 district + 3 at-large (3-yr staggered terms)
ME:portland.council_note: Mayor elected by ranked-choice voting. City Manager is chief administrative officer.

ME:lewiston.council_structure: 7 ward councilors (mayor has limited voting power)
ME:lewiston.council_note: Mayor elected separately with limited council vote. Staggered 2- and 3-year terms introduced 2025.

ME:bangor.council_note: Council elects chair who serves as mayor. City Manager is chief administrative officer.

RI:providence.council_note: Council President: Rachel Miller (Ward 13). Mayor is member of council and chief executive.
RI:providence.meeting_frequency: 1st & 3rd Thu (1st Thu only in Jul & Aug)

RI:central-falls.council_note: Smallest and most densely populated city in Rhode Island (1.29 sq mi).

NY:ithaca.charter_status: City Charter (Laws of 1908; amended 2022 to council-manager)
NY:ithaca.council_structure: 10 alderpersons (2 per ward, 5 wards, 4-year staggered terms)
NY:ithaca.council_note: Charter amended 2022 to adopt council-manager form. First city manager took office Jan 2024. Mayor remains elected at-large.

NY:rochester.charter_status: City Charter (current charter approved by referendum 1984)
NY:rochester.council_note: At-large members elected same year as mayor; district members elected 2 years later.

NJ:newark.council_note: Mayor may cast tie-breaking vote. Meetings alternate between daytime and evening.
NJ:newark.meeting_frequency: 1st Wed at 12:30 PM; 2nd Wed at 6:30 PM

NJ:jersey-city.council_structure: 6 ward + 3 at-large (4-year terms, no term limits)
NJ:jersey-city.council_note: Council president elected from among members every 2 years.

NJ:hoboken.charter_status: Faulkner Act (Optional Municipal Charter Law, Plan D since 1953)
NJ:hoboken.council_note: Council President: Ruben Ramos Jr. One of most densely populated cities in US.

PA:philadelphia.council_note: At-large members elected via limited voting; 2 seats reserved for minority party. No term limits.
PA:philadelphia.city_hall_address: 1400 John F. Kennedy Blvd, Philadelphia, PA 19107

PA:pittsburgh.council_note: All 9 members elected by district. Council meets at City-County Building.

PA:harrisburg.council_note: All members elected at-large to 4-year terms. Part-time council.

PA:state-college.council_note: Borough Manager serves as CEO. Home to Penn State University.
PA:state-college.city_hall_address: 243 S Allen St, State College, PA 16801

PA:allegheny-county.council_note: Two at-large members must be from different political parties. County Executive: Sara Innamorato.

VT:burlington.council_note: Mayor separately elected and presides over council. Progressive stronghold.

VT:montpelier.council_structure: 6 councilors from 3 districts + Mayor, all 2-year terms
VT:montpelier.council_note: City Manager is chief administrative officer. Smallest state capital by population.

VT:brattleboro.council_structure: 5 Selectboard members at-large; 150-member Representative Town Meeting as legislative body
VT:brattleboro.council_note: Town Manager handles day-to-day operations. RTM meets annually.

VT:middlebury.council_note: Town Manager appointed by Selectboard. Shire town (county seat) of Addison County.

VT:winooski.council_structure: 1 Mayor (3-year term) + 4 Councilors (2-year terms), all at-large
VT:winooski.council_note: City Manager implements policies. Most densely populated municipality in northern New England.

NH:concord.council_note: City Manager is chief executive officer. State capital.

NH:portsmouth.council_structure: 9 at-large, 2-year terms; top vote-getter becomes Mayor
NH:portsmouth.council_note: City Manager hired by council for day-to-day administration.

NH:hanover.council_structure: 5 Selectboard members; Town Manager handles daily administration
NH:hanover.council_note: Open Town Meeting is legislative body, held annually. Home to Dartmouth College.

NH:keene.council_structure: 10 ward councilors (2 per ward) + 5 at-large + Mayor as chair
NH:keene.council_note: Mayor sets agenda and chairs meetings but only votes in ties. City Manager handles daily operations.

NH:durham.council_note: Town Administrator handles day-to-day operations. Home to University of New Hampshire.

NC:charlotte.council_note: Mayor votes only in ties; has veto power. 2-year terms, partisan elections.

NC:durham.council_note: Ward members reside in ward but elected citywide. Nonpartisan elections.

NC:asheville.council_note: All seats at-large; nonpartisan; 4-year staggered terms.

NC:chapel-hill.council_note: All seats at-large; nonpartisan; mayor 2-year terms; council 4-year staggered.
NC:chapel-hill.city_hall_address: 405 Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Chapel Hill, NC 27514

NC:carrboro.council_note: Formerly Board of Aldermen, renamed Town Council in 2019. Staggered 4-year terms.

VA:richmond.charter_status: VA Independent City Charter (Dillon's Rule state)
VA:richmond.council_note: Strong mayor system; mayor is chief executive. Council elects president and vice president.

VA:alexandria.charter_status: VA Independent City Charter (Dillon's Rule state)
VA:alexandria.council_note: Council-manager form adopted by referendum in 1921. Mayor has no veto power.

VA:charlottesville.council_note: Council elects Mayor and Vice Mayor from its members. Nonpartisan elections.

VA:norfolk.charter_status: VA Independent City Charter (Dillon's Rule state)
VA:norfolk.council_note: Mayor elected at-large since 2006 charter change. 4-year terms.

VA:virginia-beach.charter_status: VA Independent City Charter (Dillon's Rule state)
VA:virginia-beach.council_note: Changed to 10-district system in 2022 per federal court order. 4-year staggered terms.

VA:arlington-county.charter_status: County Manager Plan (first in U.S., adopted 1930)

VA:arlington-county.meeting_frequency: Regular meetings on Saturdays monthly; recessed meetings on Tuesdays

MD:baltimore-city.charter_status: Home Rule Charter (Article XI of MD Constitution)

DE:newark.council_note: University of Delaware community. Council 2-year terms; mayor 3-year term.

DE:dover.council_structure: 8 district (2 per district from 4 districts) + 1 at-large
DE:dover.council_note: State capital. City Manager is chief administrative officer.

DE:new-castle-county.council_note: Council doubled from 7 to 13 members in 2004.

SC:columbia.council_note: State capital. City Manager is chief executive officer.

SC:greenville.council_note: Council-Manager system since 1976. Mayor Knox White longest-serving (since 1995).

SC:north-charleston.council_note: Third-most populous city in SC. Mayor Reggie Burgess first Black mayor (2024).
SC:north-charleston.city_hall_address: 2500 City Hall Ln, North Charleston, SC 29406

AK:juneau.council_note: Alaska state capital; consolidated city-borough since 1970.

AL:birmingham.council_structure: Strong Mayor-Council · 9 members elected by district; 4-year terms

AR:little-rock.council_note: Arkansas state capital. Board called Board of Directors.

MI:ferndale.council_note: Hub of Metro Detroit's LGBTQ+ community.

NM:santa-fe.council_note: New Mexico state capital. Mayor votes on all council matters.

NV:henderson.council_note: Second-most populous city in Nevada. Charter established 1965.

NV:reno.council_note: Restructured from 5 wards to 6 wards after 2024 election.

TN:knoxville.council_structure: Strong Mayor + 9 council members (6 district + 3 at-large)

TX:denton.council_note: Incorporated 1866. Work sessions precede regular meetings.

TX:el-paso.council_note: 6th largest city in Texas. Current charter approved 2004.
TX:el-paso.city_hall_address: 300 N. Campbell Street, El Paso, TX 79901

UT:moab.council_note: Major tourism hub near Arches & Canyonlands National Parks.

UT:park-city.council_note: Mayor votes only in case of a tie. Staggered 4-year terms.

UT:salt-lake-county.council_structure: Legislative Council · 6 district (4-yr) + 3 at-large (6-yr); separate elected Mayor
UT:salt-lake-county.council_note: Home rule county government. Most populous county in Utah.
UT:salt-lake-county.city_hall_address: 2001 South State Street, Salt Lake City, UT 84190

OH:columbus.council_structure: Strong Mayor-Council · 9 members elected at-large tied to residential districts

OK:norman.council_note: City Manager is chief administrative officer.

OK:oklahoma-city.city_hall_address: 200 N Walker Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73102

OR:eugene.council_structure: Council-Manager · Mayor (no vote except ties) + 8 ward-elected councilors

OR:portland.council_structure: Mayor-Council · 12 councilors from 4 districts (3 per district) via ranked-choice voting

OR:salem.council_note: Oregon state capital. Governed by 1996 Salem Charter.

IL:oak-park.council_structure: Village President at-large + 6 Trustees at-large; Village Manager appointed
IL:oak-park.council_note: Organized as a Village. Village Manager form since 1951.

IN:gary.council_note: Located in Lake County, 25 miles from downtown Chicago.

WA:spokane.council_note: Shifted to strong mayor form in 2001.
WA:spokane.city_hall_address: 808 W Spokane Falls Blvd, Spokane, WA 99201
